# SimDrive

SimDrive is an MCP-native iOS automation toolkit for reproducing and
validating iOS bugs in 60 seconds with Claude (or any MCP-compatible agent).
Your agent drives the iOS Simulator or a paired real device via 32 vision-first
tools, captures the failure as a deterministic recording, and replays that
recording in CI with zero AI cost on subsequent runs.

Distribution: PyPI package `simdrive` (also the MCP server command). The hero
use case is **bug reproduction and validation** — paste a Linear/Jira ticket
into your agent, watch it walk the steps, attach the captured recording to
the PR. Record-time AI cost is borne by your MCP client (BYOK); replay is free
forever after.

Licensing: 14-day full-feature trial issued locally by `simdrive trial start
--email <addr>` (Ed25519-signed, machine-locked). After 14 days the MCP tool
surface is paywalled — purchase a Pro / Team / Enterprise license at
<https://simdrive.dev/pricing> and install it with `simdrive auth
<license-key>`. Trial CLI commands (`trial`, `license`, `auth`) themselves
never require a license; everything else does.

> Repo note: the GitHub repository is currently `SyncTek-LLC/specterqa-ios`;
> rename to `SyncTek-LLC/simdrive` is pending. The PyPI package name is
> **`simdrive`**.

## Install + activate

```bash
pip install simdrive
simdrive trial start --email you@example.com
# 14 days full access, then:
simdrive auth <license-key>
```

Requires: macOS, Xcode 15+, Python 3.10+.

## Use via MCP (Claude Code / Claude Desktop / Cursor)

Add to `.claude/mcp.json` (Claude Code), `claude_desktop_config.json` (Claude
Desktop), or your Cursor MCP config:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "simdrive": {
      "command": "simdrive"
    }
  }
}
```

Then in your agent:

> "Use simdrive to reproduce Linear ENG-1247 — sign-in fails on iPhone 17 /
> iOS 26.3. Try test@example.com and capture the error."

Your agent drives the simulator, captures every action, and saves a
deterministic recording. Future CI runs replay that recording without
calling the agent.

## Minimum-viable session (5 calls)

```python
session_start(bundle_id="com.example.app")
observe()                                  # observe initial screen
tap(label="Sign In")                       # interact
observe()                                  # verify state
record_stop(session_id="...", name="signin-smoke")  # save replay
session_end(session_id="...")              # clean up
```

## MCP Tool Registry (32 tools — canonical)

The exhaustive machine-readable list lives in
`simdrive/src/simdrive/server.py::_TOOLS`. The categorized human-readable
surface lives in `docs/MCP_TOOL_SURFACE.md`. The 32 tools exposed by the MCP
server are:

- `session_start` — Start a SimDrive session on a booted iOS Simulator (or
  opted-in physical device).
- `session_end` — Stop the runner and clean up the session.
- `session_status` — Report active sessions, modes, and version.
- `observe` — Capture an annotated screenshot + element list (vision-first
  primitive).
- `tap` — Tap by mark id, label, identifier, or raw coordinates.
- `swipe` — Swipe in a direction or between two points.
- `type_text` — Type into a focused text field.
- `press_key` — Press a named keyboard key.
- `record_start` — Begin a new recording (clears the step buffer).
- `record_stop` — Save the current recording and clear the buffer.
- `replay` — Run a saved recording end-to-end against a booted target.
- `list_devices` — List booted iOS simulators (UDID, name, runtime, state).
- `logs` — Get recent app console logs from the iOS Simulator.
- `perf` — Real-time CPU / RSS / thread count for the app under test.
- `perf_baseline` — Capture a metrics baseline.
- `perf_compare` — Compare current metrics against the baseline.
- `memory` — Detailed memory breakdown via the macOS `footprint` tool.
- `doctor` — Environment readiness: Xcode, simulator runtimes, booted
  devices, runner build status.
- `app_state` — App lifecycle state (foreground, background, suspended).
- `apps` — List apps installed on a booted simulator.
- `crashes` — App crashes since the session started.
- `dismiss_first_launch_alerts` — Dismiss system permission alerts at first
  launch.
- `pre_grant_permissions` — Pre-grant iOS app permissions before launch.
- `set_appearance` — Toggle dark or light mode on the iOS Simulator.
- `dismiss_sheet` — Dismiss a presented sheet by swiping down.
- `list_replays` — List saved recordings with their names, step counts, and
  timestamps.
- `validate_replay` — Parse and validate a saved recording without
  executing it.
- `version` — Return the installed SimDrive version.
- `clear_field` — Clear a text field.
- `load_journey` — Load a recorded journey for replay.
- `lint_recordings` — Lint saved recordings for state-contract drift.
- `migrate_recording` — Migrate a recording from an older schema to the
  current one.

Every tool requires a valid license. Without one, every call returns a
structured ``license_required`` envelope including the pricing URL and
copy-pasteable command hints to start a trial or install a paid key.

## Use via CLI

```bash
simdrive --version           # verify install
simdrive                     # start the MCP server (stdio)
simdrive trial start --email you@example.com
simdrive auth <license-key>  # install a paid key
simdrive license show        # show current license state
```

## Pricing

| Plan | Price | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| Trial | Free, 14 days | All Pro features, machine-locked |
| Pro | $29 / mo | One seat |
| Team | $99 / seat / mo | Multi-seat |
| Enterprise | Contact | Self-hosted licensing, SLA |

Pricing + ROI calculator: <https://simdrive.dev/pricing>
